154	/-! ## §1. The substrate tensor-product operation -/
155	
156	/-- **The substrate operation `⊗` on `Scheme.Modules X`.**
157	
158	For a scheme `X` and `M, N : X.Modules`, the tensor product
159	`M ⊗_X N : X.Modules` is the sheafification of the presheaf-of-modules tensor
160	product `PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` of the underlying presheaves of
161	`M` and `N` (affine-locally `(M ⊗_X N)(Spec A) = M(Spec A) ⊗_A N(Spec A)`).
162	
163	Per blueprint `def:scheme_modules_tensorobj`. The body lifts
164	`PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` through the sheafification functor on
165	the small Zariski site of `X` (fully defined, no `sorry`). -/
166	noncomputable def tensorObj {X : Scheme.{u}} (M N : X.Modules) : X.Modules :=
167	  ((PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).obj
168	      (PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj (R := X.presheaf) M.val N.val) :
169	    SheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf)
170	
171	/-- **Functoriality of `⊗_X`.**
172	
173	A pair of morphisms `f : M ⟶ M'` and `g : N ⟶ N'` in `X.Modules` determines a
174	morphism `f ⊗ g : tensorObj M N ⟶ tensorObj M' N'`, compatible with identities
175	and composition; the assignment `(M, N) ↦ tensorObj M N` thereby extends to a
176	bifunctor `X.Modules × X.Modules ⥤ X.Modules` natural in both arguments.
177	
178	Per blueprint `lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality`. The body inherits
179	the morphism action from `PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` under
180	sheafification (fully defined, no `sorry`). -/
181	noncomputable def tensorObj_functoriality {X : Scheme.{u}} {M M' N N' : X.Modules}
182	    (f : M ⟶ M') (g : N ⟶ N') : tensorObj M N ⟶ tensorObj M' N' :=
183	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).map
184	    (MonoidalCategory.tensorHom
185	      (C := _root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) f.val g.val)
186	
187	/-- **`⊗`-invertibility of an `𝒪_X`-module.** (Blueprint
188	`def:scheme_modules_isinvertible`.) `M : X.Modules` is `⊗`-invertible when it
189	admits a tensor inverse: an object `N` with `M ⊗_X N ≅ 𝒪_X`, where
190	`𝒪_X = SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf` is the designated unit. This is the
191	scheme-level analogue of Mathlib's `Module.Invertible`; the predicate carries its
192	inverse witness existentially, so the dual needed by the relative Picard group
193	law is definitional. -/
194	def IsInvertible {X : Scheme.{u}} (M : X.Modules) : Prop :=
195	  ∃ N : X.Modules, Nonempty (tensorObj M N ≅ SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)
196	
197	/-- **The sheaf-level dual `M^∨ := ℋom_{𝒪_X}(M, 𝒪_X)`** of an `𝒪_X`-module.
198	
199	For a scheme `X` and `M : X.Modules`, the dual `dual M : X.Modules` is the
200	sheafification of the presheaf-of-modules dual `PresheafOfModules.dual` of the
201	underlying presheaf of `M` (the internal hom into the structure presheaf,
202	`M^∨(U) = ℋom_{𝒪_X|_U}(M|_U, 𝒪_X|_U)`).
203	
204	Construction = the **exact dual analogue of `tensorObj`** (this file, `tensorObj`):
205	apply the sheafification functor `PresheafOfModules.sheafification (𝟙 …)` on the
206	small Zariski site of `X` to the (axiom-clean, sub-step-3) presheaf dual
207	`PresheafOfModules.dual M.val`. The scheme's structure presheaf `X.presheaf` is
208	`CommRingCat`-valued over the single-universe topological site `Opens X`, hence is
209	exactly the base `R₀ : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}` that `PresheafOfModules.dual`
210	requires (the value `M^∨(U) = M|_U ⟶ R|_U` is an `R(U)`-module, needing
211	commutativity) — no CommRingCat/RingCat re-bridging is needed, since
212	`tensorObj` already takes `(R := X.presheaf)` over the same CommRingCat presheaf
213	and `X.ringCatSheaf.val = X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat` definitionally.
214	
215	The sheafification functor already lands in `SheafOfModules`, so no manual
216	`Presheaf.IsSheaf` / sheaf-condition descent is needed (sheafifying an already-sheaf
217	gives an iso object; this is the file's convention, matching `tensorObj`).
218	
219	Per blueprint `lem:internal_hom_isSheaf` (§`sec:tensorobj_dual_infra`); Stacks
220	tags 01CM (internal hom into a sheaf is a sheaf) / 01CR item 2. This is the
221	`⊗`-inverse candidate of an invertible sheaf, feeding `exists_tensorObj_inverse`. -/
222	noncomputable def dual {X : Scheme.{u}} (M : X.Modules) : X.Modules :=
223	  ((PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).obj
224	      (PresheafOfModules.dual (R₀ := X.presheaf) M.val) :
225	    SheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf)
226	
227	/-- **The sheaf-level dual is contravariantly functorial in isomorphisms.** An isomorphism
228	`e : M ≅ M'` in `X.Modules` induces `dual M' ≅ dual M`, obtained by sheafifying the presheaf-level
229	dual iso `PresheafOfModules.dualIsoOfIso` of the underlying presheaf isomorphism. This is the
230	reusable "dual respects isos" ingredient (the dual analogue of `tensorObjIsoOfIso`) feeding the
231	assembly of `dual_isLocallyTrivial`: a trivialisation `L.restrict f ≅ 𝒪` yields, contravariantly,
232	`dual 𝒪 ≅ dual (L.restrict f)`. -/
233	noncomputable def dualIsoOfIso {X : Scheme.{u}} {M M' : X.Modules} (e : M ≅ M') :
234	    dual M' ≅ dual M :=
235	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).mapIso
236	    (PresheafOfModules.dualIsoOfIso (R₀ := X.presheaf)
237	      ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).mapIso e))
238	
239	/-! ## §2. (Off the critical path) the full monoidal-category structure
240	
241	iter-206 PIVOT: the full `MonoidalCategory (X.Modules)` instance is
242	**deliberately not built**. Per blueprint `rem:scheme_modules_monoidal_off_path`,
243	the relative Picard group law is a structure on *isomorphism classes* of line
244	bundles — every group axiom is a `Nonempty (… ≅ …)` proposition, so no monoidal
245	coherence (pentagon/triangle/hexagon) and no `MonoidalClosed` structure is ever
246	consumed. The earlier `monoidalCategory := sorry` instance (and the two
247	localization-transport supplements `isMonoidal_W_of_whiskerLeft`,
248	`monoidalCategoryOfIsMonoidalW`) routed through the verified-absent
249	`MonoidalClosed (PresheafOfModules R₀)` wall; they are removed here. The group
250	law is built directly on the line-bundle subcategory from the four
251	existence-of-iso lemmas below, mirroring Mathlib's `CommRing.Pic`. -/
252	
253	/-! ## §3. The lift through `LineBundle.OnProduct` (PUSH-BEYOND supporting lemmas)
254	
255	The following helper lemmas record the lift of the substrate to the
256	locally-trivial subcategory used by the relative Picard consumer. They are not
257	`\lean{...}`-pinned in the blueprint (their statements are descriptive); the
258	typed signatures here scaffold the iter-203+ bodies. -/
259	
260	/-- **The substrate operation respects isomorphisms in both arguments.**
261	
262	A pair of isomorphisms `e : M ≅ M'` and `e' : N ≅ N'` in `X.Modules` induces an
263	isomorphism `tensorObj M N ≅ tensorObj M' N'`, obtained by sheafifying the
264	tensor product (in the presheaf-of-modules monoidal category) of the underlying
265	presheaf isomorphisms `e.val`, `e'.val`. Its `hom` is
266	`tensorObj_functoriality e.hom e'.hom`. This is the reusable functor-of-isos
267	ingredient feeding `tensorObj_isLocallyTrivial`. -/
268	noncomputable def tensorObjIsoOfIso {X : Scheme.{u}} {M M' N N' : X.Modules}
269	    (e : M ≅ M') (e' : N ≅ N') : tensorObj M N ≅ tensorObj M' N' :=
270	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).mapIso
271	    (MonoidalCategory.tensorIso
272	      (C := _root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))
273	      ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).mapIso e)
274	      ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).mapIso e'))
275	
276	/-- **The substrate tensor of the structure sheaf with itself is the structure
277	sheaf.**
278	
279	`tensorObj 𝒪_X 𝒪_X ≅ 𝒪_X`, where `𝒪_X = SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf` is the
280	designated unit object. Built from the presheaf-level left unitor
281	`λ_ (𝟙_)` (the unit of the `PresheafOfModules` monoidal category is exactly
282	`SheafOfModules.unit.val`) under sheafification, composed with the
283	sheafification-adjunction counit isomorphism on the (already-sheaf) unit. -/
284	noncomputable def tensorObj_unit_iso {X : Scheme.{u}} :
285	    tensorObj (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)
286	      ≅ SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf :=
287	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).mapIso
288	      (λ_ (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))) ≪≫
289	    (asIso (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).counit).app
290	      (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)
291	
292	/-- **Left unitor for `⊗_X`.** `𝒪_X ⊗_X M ≅ M`. (Blueprint
293	`lem:tensorobj_unit_iso`, left half, `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_left_unitor`.)
294	Sheafification of the presheaf-level left unitor `λ_ M.val`, composed with the
295	sheafification counit identifying `sheafification M.val` with the (already-sheaf)
296	`M`. The cheap `mapIso` pattern; uses no abstract pullback. -/
297	noncomputable def tensorObj_left_unitor {X : Scheme.{u}} (M : X.Modules) :
298	    tensorObj (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) M ≅ M :=
299	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).mapIso
300	      ((PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategoryStruct (R := X.presheaf)).leftUnitor M.val) ≪≫
301	    (asIso (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).counit).app M
302	
303	/-- **Right unitor for `⊗_X`.** `M ⊗_X 𝒪_X ≅ M`. (Blueprint
304	`lem:tensorobj_unit_iso`, right half, `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_right_unitor`.)
305	Sheafification of the presheaf-level right unitor `ρ_ M.val`, composed with the
306	sheafification counit. -/
307	noncomputable def tensorObj_right_unitor {X : Scheme.{u}} (M : X.Modules) :
308	    tensorObj M (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) ≅ M :=
309	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).mapIso
310	      ((PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategoryStruct (R := X.presheaf)).rightUnitor M.val) ≪≫
311	    (asIso (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).counit).app M
312	
313	/-- **Braiding for `⊗_X`.** `M ⊗_X N ≅ N ⊗_X M`. (Blueprint
314	`lem:tensorobj_comm_iso`, `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_braiding`.)
315	The presheaf-of-modules monoidal category is symmetric; its braiding `β_ M.val
316	N.val` sheafifies to the asserted isomorphism by the cheap `mapIso` pattern. -/
317	noncomputable def tensorObj_braiding {X : Scheme.{u}} (M N : X.Modules) :
318	    tensorObj M N ≅ tensorObj N M :=
319	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).mapIso
320	    (BraidedCategory.braiding
321	      (C := _root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) M.val N.val)
322	
323	/-- **Associator for `⊗_X`.** (Blueprint
324	`lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso`, `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_assoc_iso`.)
325	For arbitrary `M, N, P : X.Modules` there is an isomorphism
326	`(M ⊗_X N) ⊗_X P ≅ M ⊗_X (N ⊗_X P)`. This is the objectwise existence-of-iso datum the
327	group law consumes (associativity as a `Nonempty (… ≅ …)`).
328	
329	**UNCONDITIONAL and axiom-clean (iter-238 ROUTE (d)).** No flatness or local-triviality
330	hypothesis is used: the earlier flatness route (`W_whisker{Right,Left}_of_flat`, needing
331	sectionwise flatness — false for a general line bundle on a non-affine open) is RETIRED.
332	Writing `a = PresheafOfModules.sheafification (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)` and `η` the
333	sheafification-adjunction unit, the three-step composite is:
334	  1. `a(η_{M.val ⊗ᵖ N.val} ▷ P.val)` is iso, giving
335	     `(M ⊗ N) ⊗ P ≅ a((M.val⊗N.val) ⊗ P.val)`;
336	  2. `a.mapIso α : a((M.val⊗N.val)⊗P.val) ≅ a(M.val⊗(N.val⊗P.val))`, `α` the
337	     presheaf-of-modules associator;
338	  3. `a(M.val ◁ η_{N.val ⊗ᵖ P.val})` is iso, giving
339	     `a(M.val⊗(N.val⊗P.val)) ≅ M ⊗ (N ⊗ P)`.
340	Steps 1/3 invert the whiskered sheafification unit via the flatness-free
341	`PresheafOfModules.W_whisker{Right,Left}_of_W` (η = `toSheafify ∈ J.W`, and `J.W` is
342	stable under whiskering) together with `isIso_sheafification_map_of_W` (the sheafification
343	functor IS the localization at `J.W.inverseImage (toPresheaf _)`). The defeq carrier bridge
344	`X.ringCatSheaf.val = X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat` is handled by the leading
345	`letI instMS` below. -/
346	noncomputable def tensorObj_assoc_iso {X : Scheme.{u}} {M N P : X.Modules} :
347	    tensorObj (tensorObj M N) P ≅ tensorObj M (tensorObj N P) := by
348	  -- UNCONDITIONAL (iter-238, step 0): the locally-trivial hypotheses are dropped —
349	  -- the body never consumed them (the whiskered-unit localizer fact holds for
350	  -- arbitrary modules under ROUTE (d)). Matches the blueprint `lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso`
351	  -- framed unconditional and enables `tensorObj_assoc_iso_invertible`.
352	  -- Bridge the monoidal structure across the `rfl`-defeq carrier
353	  -- `Sheaf.val X.ringCatSheaf = X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat`.
354	  letI instMS : MonoidalCategoryStruct (_root_.PresheafOfModules (Sheaf.val X.ringCatSheaf)) :=
355	    inferInstanceAs (MonoidalCategoryStruct
356	      (_root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))
357	  set a := PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val) with ha
358	  -- Underlying presheaf tensors and the sheafification unit `η = toSheafify`.
359	  set MN := PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj (R := X.presheaf) M.val N.val with hMN
360	  set NP := PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj (R := X.presheaf) N.val P.val with hNP
361	  set η := (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction
362	    (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).unit with hη
363	  -- The two whiskered-unit localizer facts (ROUTE (d), via `W_whisker{Right,Left}_of_W`).
364	  -- `η_A = toSheafify` lies in `J.W` (`W_toSheafify`).
365	  have hηMN : (Opens.grothendieckTopology X).W
366	      ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _).map (η.app MN)) := by
367	    rw [hη, PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf_map_sheafificationAdjunction_unit_app]
368	    exact CategoryTheory.GrothendieckTopology.W_toSheafify _ _
369	  have hηNP : (Opens.grothendieckTopology X).W
370	      ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _).map (η.app NP)) := by
371	    rw [hη, PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf_map_sheafificationAdjunction_unit_app]
372	    exact CategoryTheory.GrothendieckTopology.W_toSheafify _ _
373	  have hW1 : (Opens.grothendieckTopology X).W
374	      ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _).map (η.app MN ▷ P.val)) :=
375	    PresheafOfModules.W_whiskerRight_of_W (R := X.presheaf) P.val (η.app MN) hηMN
376	  have hW3 : (Opens.grothendieckTopology X).W
377	      ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _).map (M.val ◁ η.app NP)) :=
378	    PresheafOfModules.W_whiskerLeft_of_W (R := X.presheaf) M.val (η.app NP) hηNP
379	  -- Steps 1 and 3: the sheafification functor inverts the whiskered units.
380	  have hi1 : IsIso (a.map (η.app MN ▷ P.val)) :=
381	    PresheafOfModules.isIso_sheafification_map_of_W (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val) _ hW1
382	  have hi3 : IsIso (a.map (M.val ◁ η.app NP)) :=
383	    PresheafOfModules.isIso_sheafification_map_of_W (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val) _ hW3
384	  -- Step 2: the presheaf-of-modules associator, transported under `a`.
385	  have e2 := a.mapIso
386	    ((PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategoryStruct (R := X.presheaf)).associator M.val N.val P.val)
387	  exact (@asIso _ _ _ _ _ hi1).symm ≪≫ e2 ≪≫ (@asIso _ _ _ _ _ hi3)
388	
389	/-- **Refining a trivialisation to a smaller open.** If `M` is trivialised on an
390	open `U` (`M.restrict U.ι ≅ 𝒪_U`), it is trivialised on every open `W ≤ U`.
391	
392	The chart-chase is identical in spirit to `LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial.pullback`:
393	factor `W.ι = (X.homOfLE hWU) ≫ U.ι`, transport through `restrictFunctorCongr`
394	and `restrictFunctorComp` to identify `M.restrict W.ι` with
395	`(M.restrict U.ι).restrict (X.homOfLE hWU)`, restrict the given trivialisation
396	`e` along that open immersion, and identify the restricted unit with the unit
397	via `restrictFunctorIsoPullback` + `pullbackObjUnitToUnit` (an isomorphism
398	because the open immersion's `Opens.map` is `Final`). -/
399	noncomputable def restrictIsoUnitOfLE {X : Scheme.{u}} {M : X.Modules} {U W : X.Opens}
400	    (hWU : W ≤ U)
401	    (e : M.restrict U.ι ≅ SheafOfModules.unit (U : Scheme).ringCatSheaf) :
402	    M.restrict W.ι ≅ SheafOfModules.unit (W : Scheme).ringCatSheaf := by
403	  have hWU' : W ≤ (𝟙 X) ⁻¹ᵁ U := hWU
404	  set j : (W : Scheme) ⟶ (U : Scheme) := Scheme.Hom.resLE (𝟙 X) U W hWU' with hj
405	  have hjι : j ≫ U.ι = W.ι := by rw [hj, Scheme.Hom.resLE_comp_ι, Category.comp_id]
406	  haveI : (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map j.base).Final :=
407	    CategoryTheory.final_of_representablyFlat _
408	  -- M.restrict W.ι ≅ (pullback W.ι).obj M
409	  refine (Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback W.ι).app M ≪≫ ?_
410	  -- ≅ (pullback (j ≫ U.ι)).obj M
411	  refine (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr hjι.symm).app M ≪≫ ?_
412	  -- ≅ (pullback j).obj ((pullback U.ι).obj M)
413	  refine (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp j U.ι).symm.app M ≪≫ ?_
414	  -- ≅ (pullback j).obj (M.restrict U.ι)
415	  refine (Scheme.Modules.pullback j).mapIso
416	    ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback U.ι).symm.app M) ≪≫ ?_
417	  -- ≅ (pullback j).obj 𝒪_U
418	  refine (Scheme.Modules.pullback j).mapIso e ≪≫ ?_
419	  -- ≅ 𝒪_W
420	  haveI hI : IsIso (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit j.toRingCatSheafHom) := inferInstance
421	  exact @asIso _ _ _ _ _ hI

423	/-- **Substrate tensor commutes with restriction along an open immersion.**
424	
425	For an open immersion `f : Y ⟶ X` and `M N : X.Modules`,
426	`(tensorObj M N).restrict f ≅ tensorObj (M.restrict f) (N.restrict f)`.
427	
428	This is the single substrate linchpin of `A.1.c.SubT` — **CLOSED, axiom-clean**
429	(iter-217). It says the substrate `⊗` (sheafification of the presheaf-of-modules
430	tensor) commutes with the restriction functor along an open immersion. The proof
431	is the blueprint's four-step composite:
432	  Step 1 (`restrictFunctorIsoPullback`): reduce `restrict` to the abstract pullback.
433	  Step 2 (`SheafOfModules.sheafificationCompPullback`): move the pullback inside the
434	    sheafification (sheafification commutes with pullback).
435	  Step 3: strip the outer sheafification (`.mapIso`), descending to the presheaf goal
436	    `(pullback φ).obj (M.val ⊗ₚ N.val) ≅ (M.restrict f).val ⊗ₚ (N.restrict f).val`.
437	  Step 4: close that presheaf goal by **H1 ∘ H2**:
438	    • H1 (the sole Mathlib-ABSENT ingredient, BUILT this iter): the presheaf-level iso
439	      `pushforward β ≅ pullback φ`, obtained from the de-sheafified presheaf
440	      `PresheafOfModules.pushforwardPushforwardAdj` (adjunction along the open-immersion
441	      pair `f.opensFunctor ⊣ Opens.map f.base`) against the existing
442	      `pullbackPushforwardAdjunction` via `Adjunction.leftAdjointUniq`. Here `β` is the
443	      `restrictFunctor` structure map, so `(M.restrict f).val = (pushforward β).obj M.val`
444	      definitionally.
445	    • H2 (strong-monoidal tensorator): `pushforward β = pushforward₀ ⋙ restrictScalars β`
446	      with `β` sectionwise the open-immersion ring ISO `f.appIso`, so `restrictScalars β`
447	      is STRONG monoidal (`restrictScalarsMonoidalOfBijective`, resting on the closed
448	      `restrictScalarsRingIsoTensorEquiv` / `restrictScalars_isIso_{μ,ε}`); the composite
449	      `μIso` is the tensorator.
450	The superseded `Localization.Monoidal` / `J.W.IsMonoidal` route is NOT used. -/
451	noncomputable def tensorObj_restrict_iso {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ X)
452	    [IsOpenImmersion f] (M N : X.Modules) :
453	    (tensorObj M N).restrict f ≅ tensorObj (M.restrict f) (N.restrict f) := by
454	  -- Step 1. Reduce `restrict` to `pullback` along the open immersion `f`
455	  -- (`restrictFunctorIsoPullback`, Mathlib).
456	  refine (Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback f).app (tensorObj M N) ≪≫ ?_
457	  -- Step 2. **Sheafification commutes with pullback.** `tensorObj M N` is, by
458	  -- definition, `sheafification.obj (PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj
459	  -- M.val N.val)`, so the genuine Mathlib lemma
460	  -- `SheafOfModules.sheafificationCompPullback`
461	  -- (`Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Sheaf.PullbackContinuous`,
462	  -- `sheafification ⋙ pullback φ ≅ PresheafOfModules.pullback φ.hom ⋙
463	  -- sheafification`) moves the pullback *inside* the sheafification. This
464	  -- discharges half (ii) of the original obstruction (sheafification commuting
465	  -- with pullback). After it the goal is the purely **presheaf-level** residual
466	  -- `sheafify ((PresheafOfModules.pullback φ.hom).obj (M.val ⊗ N.val))
467	  --    ≅ (M.restrict f).tensorObj (N.restrict f)`.
468	  refine (SheafOfModules.sheafificationCompPullback f.toRingCatSheafHom).app
469	      (PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj M.val N.val) ≪≫ ?_
470	  -- Step 3. **Strip the outer sheafification.** Both sides are
471	  -- `PresheafOfModules.sheafification (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.obj)` applied to a
472	  -- presheaf-of-modules: the LHS to `(pullback φ.hom).obj (M.val ⊗ₚ N.val)`, and
473	  -- the RHS `(M.restrict f).tensorObj (N.restrict f)` *by definition* to
474	  -- `(M.restrict f).val ⊗ₚ (N.restrict f).val`. So it suffices to give the
475	  -- comparison at the PRESHEAF level and sheafify it. This is a genuine reduction
476	  -- step (verified: the goal below has no sheafification).
477	  refine (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := Y.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.obj)).mapIso ?_
478	  -- Step 4 (RESIDUAL CLOSURE — iter-217 H1 build). The remaining presheaf goal is
479	  --   `(PresheafOfModules.pullback φ).obj (M.val ⊗ₚ N.val)
480	  --      ≅ (M.restrict f).val ⊗ₚ (N.restrict f).val`
481	  -- where `φ = (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom f).hom` and `⊗ₚ =
482	  -- PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj`. We close it via:
483	  --  (H1, the linchpin) the presheaf-level iso `pushforward β ≅ pullback φ`, built from
484	  --      the presheaf `pushforwardPushforwardAdj` (above) against the existing
485	  --      `pullbackPushforwardAdjunction` via `leftAdjointUniq`. Here `β` is the
486	  --      open-immersion structure map of `restrictFunctor f`, so
487	  --      `(M.restrict f).val = (pushforward β).obj M.val` definitionally.
488	  --  (H2) the strong-monoidal comparison `(pushforward β).obj (A ⊗ₚ B) ≅
489	  --      (pushforward β).obj A ⊗ₚ (pushforward β).obj B`.
490	  -- `φR` (the scheme structure map) and `β` (the restrictFunctor structure map) are kept as
491	  -- `let`-bindings (zeta-transparent) so the unit/counit triangle goals below reduce; the
492	  -- open-immersion adjunction is INLINED for the same reason (a `have` would make `adj.unit`
493	  -- opaque and block the `congr` defeq, exactly as in Mathlib's sheaf-level `restrictAdjunction`).
494	  let φR := (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom f).hom
495	  -- The restrictFunctor structure map `β` (so `(M.restrict f).val = (pushforward β).obj M.val`).
496	  let α : Y.presheaf ⟶ f.opensFunctor.op ⋙ X.presheaf :=
497	    { app := fun U => (f.appIso U.unop).inv }
498	  let β : Y.ringCatSheaf.obj ⟶ f.opensFunctor.op ⋙ X.ringCatSheaf.obj :=
499	    Functor.whiskerRight α (forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)
500	  -- H1 via the presheaf pushforward-pushforward adjunction + `leftAdjointUniq`.
501	  have hadj : PresheafOfModules.pushforward β ⊣ PresheafOfModules.pushforward φR :=
502	    PresheafOfModules.pushforwardPushforwardAdj f.isOpenEmbedding.isOpenMap.adjunction β φR
503	      (by ext U x; exact congr($((f.app_appIso_inv _).symm).hom x))
504	      (by ext U x; exact congr($(f.appIso_inv_app_presheafMap U.unop) x))
505	  let H1 := hadj.leftAdjointUniq (PresheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction φR)
506	  refine (H1.app (PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj M.val N.val)).symm ≪≫ ?_
507	  -- H2: the strong-monoidal tensorator of `pushforward β = pushforward₀ ⋙ restrictScalars β`.
508	  -- `β` is sectionwise bijective (it is the `forget₂`-image of the open-immersion structure ring
509	  -- ISO `f.appIso`), so `restrictScalars β` is STRONG monoidal (`restrictScalarsMonoidalOfBijective`),
510	  -- and `pushforward₀OfCommRingCat` is `Monoidal` (Mathlib); the composite's `μIso` is the tensorator.
511	  -- It is built over the SYNTACTIC `_ ⋙ forget₂` base form (where the `MonoidalCategory` instance is
512	  -- found canonically); the result is DEFEQ to the goal — whose base `X.ringCatSheaf.obj` is only
513	  -- defeq, not syntactically, `X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂` — and `(pushforward β).obj M.val =
514	  -- (M.restrict f).val` definitionally, so `exact` closes it without any instance diamond.
515	  have hβ : ∀ U, Function.Bijective (β.app U).hom := by
516	    intro U
517	    haveI : IsIso (β.app U) :=
518	      inferInstanceAs (IsIso ((forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).map (f.appIso U.unop).inv))
519	    exact ConcreteCategory.bijective_of_isIso (β.app U)
520	  let β' : (Y.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) ⟶
521	      (f.opensFunctor.op ⋙ X.presheaf) ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat := β
522	  haveI : (PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars β').Monoidal :=
523	    PresheafOfModules.restrictScalarsMonoidalOfBijective β' hβ
524	  exact (Functor.Monoidal.μIso
525	    (PresheafOfModules.pushforward₀OfCommRingCat f.opensFunctor X.presheaf
526	      ⋙ PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars β')
527	    (M.val : _root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))
528	    (N.val : _root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))).symm

530	/-- **Tensor product of locally-trivial modules is locally trivial.**
531	
532	If `M, N : X.Modules` are locally trivial of rank one (line bundles), so is
533	their tensor product `tensorObj M N`. Per blueprint
534	`lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial`. The proof picks, for each point `x`,
535	a common affine open `W ∋ x` contained in trivialising opens `U` (for `M`) and
536	`U'` (for `N`), refines both trivialisations to `W` via `restrictIsoUnitOfLE`,
537	then transports through `tensorObj_restrict_iso`, the bifunctoriality
538	`tensorObjIsoOfIso`, and the unit isomorphism `tensorObj_unit_iso`:
539	`(M ⊗ N)|_W ≅ M|_W ⊗ N|_W ≅ 𝒪_W ⊗ 𝒪_W ≅ 𝒪_W`. The only residual gap is the
540	substrate-restriction compatibility `tensorObj_restrict_iso`. -/
541	lemma tensorObj_isLocallyTrivial {X : Scheme.{u}} {M N : X.Modules}
542	    (hM : LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial M) (hN : LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial N) :
543	    LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial (tensorObj M N) := by
544	  intro x
545	  obtain ⟨U, hxU, hU_aff, ⟨eM⟩⟩ := hM x
546	  obtain ⟨U', hxU', hU'_aff, ⟨eN⟩⟩ := hN x
547	  obtain ⟨W, hW_aff, hxW, hWsub⟩ :=
548	    exists_isAffineOpen_mem_and_subset (X := X) (x := x) (U := U ⊓ U') ⟨hxU, hxU'⟩
549	  have hWU : W ≤ U := le_trans hWsub inf_le_left
550	  have hWU' : W ≤ U' := le_trans hWsub inf_le_right
551	  refine ⟨W, hxW, hW_aff, ⟨?_⟩⟩
552	  exact tensorObj_restrict_iso W.ι M N ≪≫
553	    tensorObjIsoOfIso (restrictIsoUnitOfLE hWU eM) (restrictIsoUnitOfLE hWU' eN) ≪≫
554	    tensorObj_unit_iso

556	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — the d.2-free descent re-route (B-connector)
557	
558	The "locally-iso ⇒ iso" half of the descent assembly of `exists_tensorObj_inverse`:
559	a morphism of `𝒪_X`-modules that restricts to an isomorphism on an open
560	neighbourhood of every point is a global isomorphism. The route is the stalkwise
561	iso criterion `TopCat.Presheaf.isIso_of_stalkFunctor_map_iso` (for sheaves valued
562	in `Ab`, whose forgetful functor reflects isos and preserves limits / filtered
563	colimits) together with `Scheme.Modules.restrictStalkNatIso` (restriction along an
564	open immersion commutes with stalks). **No stalk-⊗ ("d.2") is invoked**: this is a
565	statement about a single module morphism, never about the tensor stalk. -/
566	
567	/-- **B-connector: a morphism of `𝒪_X`-modules that restricts to an isomorphism on
568	an open cover is an isomorphism.** For `φ : M ⟶ N` in `X.Modules`, if every point
569	`x` lies in an open `U x` on which the restriction `(restrictFunctor (U x).ι).map φ`
570	is an isomorphism, then `φ` is an isomorphism. This is the B-bridge of the d.2-free
571	descent re-route assembling `exists_tensorObj_inverse`. -/
572	lemma isIso_of_isIso_restrict {X : Scheme.{u}} {M N : X.Modules} (φ : M ⟶ N)
573	    (U : X → X.Opens) (hxU : ∀ x, x ∈ U x)
574	    (h : ∀ x, IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctor (U x).ι).map φ)) :
575	    IsIso φ := by
576	  -- It suffices that each stalk map of the underlying `Ab`-sheaf morphism is iso.
577	  have hst : ∀ x : X, IsIso ((TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor Ab.{u} x).map
578	      ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X).map φ)) := by
579	    intro x
580	    obtain ⟨x', hx'⟩ : ∃ x', (U x).ι x' = x := by
581	      have hmem : x ∈ (U x).ι.opensRange := by
582	        rw [Scheme.Opens.opensRange_ι]; exact hxU x
583	      exact AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Hom.mem_opensRange.mp hmem
584	    haveI : IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctor (U x).ι).map φ) := h x
585	    -- `(restrictFunctor … ⋙ toPresheaf … ⋙ stalkFunctor x').map φ` is iso (functor of an iso).
586	    haveI hFφ : IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctor (U x).ι ⋙
587	        Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf _ ⋙ TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor Ab.{u} x').map φ) := by
588	      dsimp only [Functor.comp_map]; exact Functor.map_isIso _ _
589	    -- Transport the iso across `restrictStalkNatIso` to the stalk at `(U x).ι x' = x`.
590	    have hGφ : IsIso ((TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor Ab.{u} ((U x).ι x')).map
591	        ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X).map φ)) :=
592	      (CategoryTheory.NatIso.isIso_map_iff
593	        (Scheme.Modules.restrictStalkNatIso (U x).ι x') φ).mp hFφ
594	    exact hx' ▸ hGφ
595	  -- Package as a morphism of `TopCat.Sheaf Ab X` and apply the stalkwise iso criterion.
596	  let MS : TopCat.Sheaf Ab.{u} X := ⟨M.presheaf, M.isSheaf⟩
597	  let NS : TopCat.Sheaf Ab.{u} X := ⟨N.presheaf, N.isSheaf⟩

1	# Analogy: composite-adjunction unit cocycle for `L = Q ∘ F` (sheafify ∘ pullback), recovering the functor-wrapped inner unit via a `homEquiv`/conjugate head

12	## Structural problem (abstracted)
13	Two adjunctions whose left adjoints are composites `L = Q ∘ F` (`Q` a reflective
14	localization / sheafification, `F` a base-change/pullback functor). Need the unit-cocycle for the
15	composite-of-composites: `η^{h≫f}` factors through `η^f`, `η^h`, and a nontrivial comparison 2-cell
16	`(Q∘F)(h)∘(Q∘F)(f) ≅ (Q∘F)(h≫f)`. The single sticking point: a functor-wrapped comparison factor
17	`(pullback h).map ((sheafCompPb f).hom.app P)` (`= whiskerLeft (pullback h) (comparison_f)` at a point)
18	has no transposable head, so the brick `leftAdjointUniqUnitEta_app` (= `homEquiv_leftAdjointUniq_hom_app`)
19	cannot fire to rewrite it into the inner composite-adjunction unit `B_f.unit`.
20	
21	## Failed approaches (from directive)
22	- Sectionwise `hom_ext; intro U; rfl`: composite-adjunction unit not sectionwise trivial (sheafification unit contaminates).
23	- Transposing the WHOLE tail back through `homEquiv`: circular (re-folds the R0-peel; iter-263).
24	- `Adjunction.comp_unit_app` + `unit_naturality` directly: inner factors never present a `homEquiv` head.
25	- Boundary bridge `forget_map_pushforward_map` (rfl, landed): crosses sheaf↔presheaf but supplies no head.
26

29	### Analogue: `CategoryTheory.Adjunction.conjugateEquiv_whiskerLeft` (`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Adjunction/Mates.lean:525`)  ← THE missing device
30	- **Domain**: pure category theory (mate calculus / conjugate transformations).
31	- **Same structural problem there**: how does the conjugate (= the homEquiv transpose between two
32	  left-adjoint factorizations) interact with *whiskering a comparison 2-cell by an outer functor*?
33	  Statement:
34	  ```
35	  conjugateEquiv (adj.comp adj₁) (adj.comp adj₂) (whiskerLeft L τ)
36	      = whiskerRight (conjugateEquiv adj₁ adj₂ τ) R
37	  ```
38	  i.e. the conjugate of `L`-whiskered `τ` (where `adj : L ⊣ R`) equals `R`-whiskered conjugate of `τ`.
39	- **Technique**: `ext X; ... simp [← reassoc_of% h₁, reassoc_of% h₂]` where `h₁ = (R₂⋙R).map (τ.naturality (adj.counit …))`, `h₂ = R.map (adj₂.unit_naturality (adj.counit …))`. The whole content is "slide the
40	  comparison's naturality + the outer adjunction's unit/counit triangle past the whiskering".
41	- **Mapping to project**: the project's stuck factor is `forget.map ((pullback h).map ((sheafCompPb f).hom.app P))`.
42	  Here `(pullback h)` is the LEFT adjoint of the h-adjunction `pullbackPushforwardAdjunction h : pullback h ⊣ pushforward h`,
43	  and `(pullback h).map (g.app P) = (whiskerLeft (pullback h) g).app P` for the natural transformation
44	  `g = (sheafCompPb f).hom`. Apply `conjugateEquiv_whiskerLeft` with `adj := (h-composite-adjunction)`,
45	  `adj₁/adj₂ := the two f-composite-adjunctions A_f, B_f`, `L := pullback h`, `τ := (sheafCompPb f).hom`
46	  (which is a `conjugateEquiv`-image of identity, since `sheafCompPb f = leftAdjointUniq A_f B_f` and
47	  `leftAdjointUniq = (conjugateIsoEquiv …).symm (Iso.refl _) |>.symm`, Unique.lean:36). The conclusion
48	  gives the factor a `whiskerRight (conjugateEquiv …) R` form whose conjugate/`homEquiv` head is exactly
49	  what `leftAdjointUniqUnitEta_app` consumes. The `forget`/`restrictScalars` outer wrapping is already
50	  crossed by the landed `forget_map_pushforward_map`.
51	- **Porting cost**: low–medium. One `have` mirroring `conjugateEquiv_whiskerLeft`'s 4-line proof, instantiated
52	  at the project's composite adjunctions. No new infrastructure; `conjugateEquiv_whiskerLeft`,
53	  `conjugateEquiv_comp` (Mates.lean:338), `conjugateEquiv_whiskerRight` (Mates.lean:536) are all shipped.

85	- **Domain**: category theory — localization of an adjunction along reflective localizations (the abstract
86	  shape of sheafification).
87	- **Same structural problem there**: given `adj : G ⊣ F` and localization functors `L₁, L₂` with 2-commutative
88	  squares `CatCommSq G L₁ L₂ G'` / `CatCommSq F L₂ L₁ F'` (the comparison 2-cells), the localized adjunction
89	  `G' ⊣ F'` has unit
90	  ```
91	  (adj.localization …).unit.app (L₁.obj X₁) =
92	    L₁.map (adj.unit.app X₁) ≫ (CatCommSq.iso F L₂ L₁ F').hom.app _ ≫ F'.map ((CatCommSq.iso G L₁ L₂ G').hom.app _)
93	  ```

95	  `sheafificationCompPullback`). Exactly the cocycle shape, with `Q = L₁` and `F = G`.
96	- **Technique**: the localized unit is `liftNatTrans L₁ W₁ … (whiskerRight adj.unit L₁)` (universal property of
97	  localization extends the un-localized unit). Identities about it are proved by `natTrans_ext L₁ W₁`
98	  (two transformations out of a localized category agree iff they agree after whiskering with `L₁` =
99	  `toSheafify`) — NOT sectionwise — and the comparison 2-cells slide via `(CatCommSq.iso …).naturality`.

102	  contaminates (failed approach #1) by using the localization universal property (`natTrans_ext` / the
103	  sheafification adjunction's `homEquiv`-injectivity on the sheafy side) instead of `intro U`.

.lake/packages/mathlib/Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Adjunction/Unique.lean:36:def leftAdjointUniq {F F' : C ⥤ D} {G : D ⥤ C} (adj1 : F ⊣ G) (adj2 : F' ⊣ G) : F ≅ F' :=
==== def leftAdjointUniq ====

namespace CategoryTheory.Adjunction

attribute [local simp] homEquiv_unit homEquiv_counit

/-- If `F` and `F'` are both left adjoint to `G`, then they are naturally isomorphic. -/
def leftAdjointUniq {F F' : C ⥤ D} {G : D ⥤ C} (adj1 : F ⊣ G) (adj2 : F' ⊣ G) : F ≅ F' :=
  ((conjugateIsoEquiv adj1 adj2).symm (Iso.refl G)).symm

theorem homEquiv_leftAdjointUniq_hom_app {F F' : C ⥤ D} {G : D ⥤ C} (adj1 : F ⊣ G) (adj2 : F' ⊣ G)
    (x : C) : adj1.homEquiv _ _ ((leftAdjointUniq adj1 adj2).hom.app x) = adj2.unit.app x := by

272:def conjugateEquiv : (L₂ ⟶ L₁) ≃ (R₁ ⟶ R₂) :=
282:    L₂.map ((conjugateEquiv adj₁ adj₂ α).app _) ≫ adj₂.counit.app d =
289:      ((conjugateEquiv adj₁ adj₂).symm α).app _ ≫ adj₁.counit.app d := by
290:    conv_lhs => rw [← (conjugateEquiv adj₁ adj₂).right_inv α]
291:    exact (conjugateEquiv_counit adj₁ adj₂ ((conjugateEquiv adj₁ adj₂).symm α) d)
294:theorem unit_conjugateEquiv (α : L₂ ⟶ L₁) (c : C) :
295:    adj₁.unit.app _ ≫ (conjugateEquiv adj₁ adj₂ α).app _ =
307:      adj₂.unit.app c ≫ R₂.map (((conjugateEquiv adj₁ adj₂).symm α).app _) := by
308:    conv_lhs => rw [← (conjugateEquiv adj₁ adj₂).right_inv α]
309:    exact (unit_conjugateEquiv adj₁ adj₂ ((conjugateEquiv adj₁ adj₂).symm α) c)
312:theorem conjugateEquiv_id : conjugateEquiv adj₁ adj₁ (𝟙 _) = 𝟙 _ := by
317:theorem conjugateEquiv_symm_id : (conjugateEquiv adj₁ adj₁).symm (𝟙 _) = 𝟙 _ := by
322:    (conjugateEquiv adj Adjunction.id α).app c = α.app (R.obj c) ≫ adj.counit.app c := by
326:    ((conjugateEquiv adj Adjunction.id).symm α).app c = adj.unit.app c ≫ α.app (L.obj c) := by
339:    conjugateEquiv adj₁ adj₂ α ≫ conjugateEquiv adj₂ adj₃ β =
340:      conjugateEquiv adj₁ adj₃ (β ≫ α) := by
355:    (conjugateEquiv adj₂ adj₃).symm β ≫ (conjugateEquiv adj₁ adj₂).symm α =
356:      (conjugateEquiv adj₁ adj₃).symm (α ≫ β) := by
361:    conjugateEquiv adj₁ adj₂ α ≫ conjugateEquiv adj₂ adj₁ β = 𝟙 _ := by
365:    (conjugateEquiv adj₂ adj₁).symm β ≫ (conjugateEquiv adj₁ adj₂).symm α = 𝟙 _ := by

  consume `hwr` one must first transpose the whole tail through the `(h≫f)`-composite adjunction

  pushforward side, after which `hwr` + `unit_conjugateEquiv` (Mates.lean:294) + `conjugateEquiv_comp`
  (Mates.lean:338) collapse it. Estimated ~40–60 LOC; **the device is no longer the blocker** — the
  remaining work is the transposition-setup + cocycle collapse.

  `hwr` (the conjugateEquiv_whiskerRight device).

  must precede consuming it. Not attempted to completion (the ~40–60 LOC two-layer cocycle collapse
  exceeds a fine-grained budget); the precise next step is documented in-file and above.
- **Recommendation for next pass:** target step (iii) with directive "transpose the whole tail via
  `(h≫f)`-composite `homEquiv.injective` using the CompositionIso `leftAdjointCompNatTrans_assoc`
  surjective/injective reduction, then `rw [hwr]` + `unit_conjugateEquiv` + `conjugateEquiv_comp`."
  The device (`hwr`) is already in place and verified.

You can navigate the project's dependency graph read-only to find the

It is a navigation aid only — the proof obligation and your blueprint

Active prover mode: **fine-grained**

## Your goal

Translate each sentence of the informal proof in the blueprint chapter into an atomic named Lean lemma, then prove each lemma independently. The theorem is never attacked whole. Progress is measured per-sentence, not per-theorem. If sentence themselves can be decomposed further, do so. 

## Why this matters

Provers stall on large goals because the first difficult step blocks the rest. By forcing each sentence to become its own named claim, every step either closes quickly or fails with a precise, fixable error — instead of a vague "I can't prove this big thing."

## Workflow

1. Read `PROGRESS.md` for your current objectives (read only — do not edit it).
2. **Read your blueprint chapter sentence by sentence.** For the section corresponding to your assigned theorem, list every individual mathematical claim made in the informal proof (not just top-level lemmas — every "since X", "by Y", "note that Z" is a candidate).
3. Read `task_pending.md` for prior attempts and dead ends.
4. Check the `.lean` file for `/- USER: ... -/` comments.
5. For each identified sentence:
   a. Introduce a named `private lemma` encoding that sentence's claim.
   b. Immediately attempt to prove it — using LSP tools, Mathlib search, and tactic exploration.
   c. If it closes: mark RESOLVED in your log; move on.
   d. If it does not close within reasonable effort: leave a `sorry` with the partial tactic attempt visible (never revert to bare `sorry`), naming the specific blocker; move on to the next sentence.
6. After all sentences are attempted, assemble the main theorem from the sub-lemma names (the assembly itself may use `sorry` if some sub-lemmas are still open).
7. Verify the file compiles.
8. Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.

**Write permissions**: only your assigned `.lean` file(s) and `task_results/<your_file>.md`. Do NOT edit `PROGRESS.md`, `task_pending.md`, other agents' files, or the blueprint chapter.

## Sentence identification heuristics

Read the informal proof text and extract each **atomic mathematical claim** — a sentence that, if true, advances the proof by one logical step. Examples:

- "The map φ is injective" → `lemma phi_injective : Function.Injective φ := …`
- "Since R is local, the maximal ideal is unique" → `lemma unique_maximal_of_local [IsLocalRing R] : … := …`
- "The kernel is contained in the Jacobson radical" → `lemma ker_le_jacobson : … := …`

Skip claims that are definitional (follow immediately from `unfold` or `simp`) — fold those into the first proof that needs them.

## Prove each sentence atomically

For each sub-lemma:

1. State the type precisely — it must exactly encode the blueprint sentence.
2. Try `simp`, `ring`, `omega`, `norm_num`, `exact?`, `apply?` as quick checks.
3. Use `lean_leansearch` with the mathematical content as the query.
4. If a Mathlib lemma covers it exactly: `exact MathLibLemmaName` or `apply`.
5. If it requires a short tactic proof: write it.
6. If it requires significant work: write whatever partial proof you have, then leave a scoped `sorry` at the stuck point. Do NOT revert to a bare `sorry` — keep the partial attempt visible.

**You are fully authorized to make every proof decision within your assigned file without waiting for the planner.** "I'll let the planner decide" is never valid for a tactic or approach choice — make the call, attempt it, document the outcome.

**Time budget**: spend at most a few tool calls per sentence before declaring PARTIAL and moving on. The point of this mode is coverage across all sentences, not depth on any single one.

**Comments are not progress. Code is.** If you write `-- TODO: try simp [X]` or `/- This could work via Y -/`, you must attempt it before writing the comment. Only leave a comment about an approach if you tried it and it failed with a named error.

## Protected declarations

Read `archon-protected.yaml` before touching any declaration. Do not change protected signatures.

## Completion criteria

This mode succeeds when:

- Every informal sentence has a corresponding named sub-lemma (proved or `sorry`).
- The main theorem's proof body references the sub-lemma names.
- The file compiles.

A subsequent `prove` pass targeting specific open sub-lemmas is expected.

## LSP MCP tools

The `archon-lean-lsp` server exposes Lean LSP operations as **MCP tool calls** (`mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_goal`, `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_diagnostic_messages`, etc.). Never call them as shell commands.

- First LSP action: `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_diagnostic_messages` on your file.
- Use `lean_goal` after each tactic to confirm progress.

## Search protocol

1. `lean_local_search` first.
2. `lean_leansearch` — describe the mathematical content of the sentence.
3. `lean_loogle` for simple type patterns only.
4. Never use shell `find` / `grep` to locate Mathlib theorems.

## Logging

```markdown

## wLocal_iff — fine-grained pass

### Sentence 1: "The map φ is injective" → `phi_injective`
- **Result:** RESOLVED — `exact PrimeSpectrum.comap_injective`

### Sentence 2: "The kernel is contained in the Jacobson radical" → `ker_le_jacobson`
- **Result:** PARTIAL — started from `Ideal.mem_jacobson_iff`, stuck at membership criterion
- **Dead end:** `exact?` found nothing; `Ideal.jacobson_mono` applies but needs surjectivity first
- **Next step:** prove surjectivity (sentence 4) first, then return here

### Sentence 3: "Since R is local, the maximal ideal is unique" → `unique_maximal_of_local`
- **Result:** RESOLVED — direct from `IsLocalRing.eq_maximalIdeal`

### Assembly (wLocal_iff)
- References: `phi_injective`, `ker_le_jacobson` (sorry), `unique_maximal_of_local`
- Compiles with scoped sorry on `ker_le_jacobson`.

## Summary
- 2/3 sentences closed; 1 blocked (kernel ⊆ Jacobson).
- Recommended next: `prove` targeting `ker_le_jacobson` with directive to establish surjectivity first.
```

## End-of-session handoff

**Before declaring done, run this self-review:**

1. Did I attempt every approach I wrote in a comment or TODO? If not, go back and attempt them.
2. After all sentences, did I try to fully assemble the main theorem? Even with sorry sub-lemmas, a structured assembly often reveals which sub-lemmas are actually easy.

Before writing results:

3. **Write a `## Summary` section**: N/M sentences closed, sorry count before → after, which sentences are open and exactly why.
4. **Write a `## Why I stopped` section** — be honest; only closed sub-lemma proofs count as progress. Sub-lemmas extracted with only `sorry` bodies and no proof attempt are cosmetics:
   - `Real progress`: M/N sentences closed — list which ones and sorry count before → after.
   - `Partial progress`: measurable code advance (partial proof bodies, one branch closed); list open sentences with their specific blockers (not "it's hard" — the exact type mismatch or missing ingredient).
   - `Approaches written but not attempted`: identified routes in comments or log but did not attempt — name each. Planner will re-assign with directive to attempt them.

26	## Subagents
27	
28	Subagents are descriptor files at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md` (YAML frontmatter — `name`, `description`, `write_domain`, `read_only`, `can_spawn`, `default_enabled`, optional `mandatory: [<phase>...]`, optional `dispatcher_notes` — followed by the prompt body the spawned Claude reads).
29	
30	**You do NOT need to discover subagents.** The plan and review prompts auto-inject an **Available subagents** section at the top of each invocation listing every enabled descriptor with description, write-domain, MANDATORY flag, and `dispatcher_notes`. When you decide to invoke a subagent, read its full prompt at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md`.
31	
32	**Invoke** via the generic wrapper (Bash tool, foreground):
33	
34	```
35	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
36	  --name <subagent-name> \
37	  --slug <kebab-slug> \
38	  --directive-file <path-to-directive.md> \
39	  --write-domain '<glob>'        # repeat for multiple
40	```
41	
42	The wrapper exits 0 on success, prints a one-line status, writes the report to `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`, and the Archon CLI auto-archives a copy to `logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-report.md` for the dashboard. The dispatch semaphore caps concurrent subagent processes at `loop.max_parallel`; the wrapper resolves `--parent-slug` from `ARCHON_SUBAGENT_SLUG` so nested children inherit the hierarchy.
43	
44	**Treat each dispatch as blocking — wait for its report before acting on it.** Don't *deliberately* set `run_in_background: true`. But note the wrapper is genuinely synchronous (it `subprocess.run`s the `archon subagent` CLI and only returns once the child finishes and its report is on disk), so the harness may *auto-background* a long-running dispatch and hand you a task ID immediately — that's expected and fine. When it does, await that task / poll for the report file at `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md` (the wrapper's one-line status and exit 0 confirm completion) before you read or act on the result. The thing to avoid is firing a dispatch and then continuing to plan as if it had already returned; whether the call blocked inline or was auto-backgrounded, the rule is the same: do not consume a subagent's output until its report exists.
45	
46	**Highly-recommended subagents.** A descriptor whose frontmatter sets `mandatory: [plan]` is treated as **highly recommended** for the plan phase (similarly `[review]` for the review phase). The catalog tags them `[HIGHLY RECOMMENDED]`. Dispatch each one unless you have a concrete reason to skip — when skipping, record a one-line rationale under a `## Subagent skips` section in `iter/iter-NNN/<phase>.md` (e.g. `- strategy-critic: STRATEGY.md SHA unchanged from prior iter and last verdict was SOUND`). A post-phase audit checks both signals: it silences when the subagent dispatched OR a skip rationale was recorded, and warns only when neither happened. Each subagent's `dispatcher_notes` enumerates its specific skip conditions — read them before deciding. **Subagents ship disabled by default** (`default_enabled: false`), so the rule only fires for subagents the user has explicitly listed under `subagents.enabled` in `.archon/config.json`; on a fresh project the catalog is empty and no recommended dispatch is required. (The frontmatter field is still named `mandatory: [...]` for backward compat with existing configs and descriptors — its semantic meaning is "highly recommended", not "must dispatch".)
47	
48	## Key Files & Permissions
49	
50	All Archon state files are in `.archon/`. The table below covers **phase agents** (plan, prover, review) and the **user**. Subagent permissions are NOT a fixed row — they are descriptor-driven (`write_domain` glob in the subagent's frontmatter, enforced at dispatch time). See "Subagent permissions" after the table.
51	
52	| File | Plan | Prover | Review | User |
53	|------|------|--------|--------|------|
54	| `PROGRESS.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
55	| `STRATEGY.md` | read + write | — | — | read |
56	| `USER_HINTS.md` | **loop-managed** (captured + cleared by the CLI; injected into the plan prompt) | — | — | write |
57	| `task_pending.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
58	| `task_done.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
59	| `task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect) | write (own file) | read only | read |
60	| `proof-journal/` | read | — | **write** | read |
61	| `PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | — | **write** (Knowledge Base only — session log lives in `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`) | read |
62	| `iter/iter-NNN/plan.md` | **write** (this iter only) | — | read (recent window injected) | read |
63	| `iter/iter-NNN/review.md` | read (recent window injected) | — | **write** (this iter only) | read |
64	| `iter/iter-NNN/objectives.md` | optional write | — | read | read |
65	| `archon-protected.yaml` | read | read | read | **write** |
66	| `.lean` files | — | write (own file; frozen protected signatures) | — | write (via comments) |
67	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints) | read | **write** (semantic markers: `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections — NOT `\leanok`) | read |
68	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | — | — | read |
69	| `TO_USER.md` | **write** (shared notice board) | **write** (shared notice board) | **write** (shared notice board) | read |
70	
71	### Subagent permissions
72	
73	Each subagent ships YAML frontmatter:
74	
75	- `write_domain: "<glob>"` — file pattern the subagent may write. Empty for purely read-only subagents (the dispatching call typically passes `--write-domain 'task_results/**'` so the subagent can still write its own report).
76	- `read_only: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may only write its `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`.
77	- `can_spawn: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may dispatch children whose `write_domain` is a subset of its own.
78	
79	The dispatcher enforces `write_domain` at the filesystem level: any child whose declared domain is not a subset of the parent's is rejected before the child agent starts. Reads are restricted only by the descriptor's prompt body (e.g. fresh-context critics that are told not to read PROGRESS.md / STRATEGY.md).
80	
81	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md`** is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow run by hand by the mathematician. The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. To invoke a refactor-style subagent inside the loop, write the directive to a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-directive.md` and dispatch via the generic wrapper. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise.
82	
83	## Protected Declarations
84	
85	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Rules:
86	
87	- **Plan / prover / review** agents: read-only on protected signatures. May fill proof bodies; may not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
88	- **Write-capable structural subagents** (per their `write_domain`): may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (name + signature preserved verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. They may NEVER rename / re-type / delete / re-sign a protected declaration.
89	- The YAML file itself is user-only. No agent adds or removes a declaration. The only allowed agent edit is updating an existing declaration's file path.
90	
91	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
92	
93	Two semantic markers:
94	
95	- `\leanok` — statement block: declaration is formalized (at least a `sorry` present). Proof block: proof closed, no `sorry`.
96	- `\mathlibok` — statement block: declaration is backed by Mathlib (re-export / alias); no Archon proof obligation remains.
97	
98	No marker means the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
99	
100	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between prover and review each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves.
101	
102	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations are the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
103	
104	## User Interaction
105	
106	The user provides hints in two places:
107	
108	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The Archon loop captures this file's content before each plan phase, injects it into the plan agent's prompt, and resets the file to its bundled template (an HTML-comment format guide + zero bullets) after the plan phase succeeds. The HTML-comment preamble is stripped before injection so a template-only file renders as "no hints" to the planner. The plan agent does NOT read or clear that file itself. Provers never see these hints.
109	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
110	
111	Archon talks back to the user through **`TO_USER.md`** — a single, *persistent* notice board surfaced as a UI banner. It is **shared and writable by every agent** (plan, prover, review). It is no longer cleared at the start of each review phase; content survives across iters until an agent prunes it. The discipline every writer follows:
112	
113	- **Concise**: the whole file stays ≤ 2–3 short bullets. It is a banner, not a log.
114	- **Relevant now**: before adding anything, read the current content and **delete any bullet that is no longer true at this point** (the blocker was resolved, the decision was overtaken, the credential got set). Never let it accumulate stale notices.
115	- **Notice board, never a question queue**: the loop is autonomous and may run unattended for many iters. Never write "awaiting your decision", an options menu, a "where to reply", or "default to X if no reply" — anything implying the loop is paused for a human. State decisions as *made* and blockers as *worked-around-as-far-as-possible*; the user steers asynchronously via `USER_HINTS.md`.
116	
117	## Agent Roles
118	
119	### Plan Agent
120	
121	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for full instructions.
122	- User hints, blueprint-doctor findings, recent iter sidecars, the subagent catalog, and the references summary are all pre-injected into your invocation prompt. You do not "go read" those files.
123	- Read `task_results/` to collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md`.
124	- Optionally invoke subagents (via the auto-injected catalog) before writing prover objectives. Subagent reports are auto-archived to `logs/iter-NNN/` by the Archon CLI.
125	- Write `PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round.
126	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch markers — `\leanok` is owned by the deterministic sync; `\mathlibok` / `% NOTE:` by the review agent.
127	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself.
128	
129	### Prover Agent
130	
131	- Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
132	- Your **active mode** is injected directly into your invocation prompt — read it carefully, it defines your goal, workflow, and constraints for this session.
133	- When your mode specifies `read_blueprint: true`, you are permitted to **read** your assigned blueprint chapter (`blueprint/src/chapters/<your_slug>.tex`) for the informal proof sketch. You may never write to blueprint chapters.
134	- Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
135	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — never edit another agent's file.
136	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your file for file-specific hints.
137	- Do NOT edit blueprint chapters. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker; the review agent applies them.
138	
139	### Subagents
140	
141	Subagents are descriptor-driven and discovered at runtime. The catalog is auto-injected into the plan and review prompts each phase (see "Subagents" above for the wrapper invocation). Each subagent reads only what its directive points at — never `PROGRESS.md` / `STRATEGY.md` / phase-agent state — and writes a self-contained report at `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`. Descriptors with `can_spawn: true` may dispatch children (subject to the global `loop.max_parallel` cap).
142	
143	### Review Agent
144	
145	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for full instructions.
146	- Read `proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data.
147	- Write the session journal to `proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (`summary.md`, `milestones.jsonl`, `recommendations.md`).
148	- Update `PROJECT_STATUS.md` (Knowledge Base only — session narrative goes to `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`).
149	- Maintain the semantic blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE:` annotations, stale `\notready` removal. Do NOT touch `\leanok`.
150	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify `PROGRESS.md`.
151	
152	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
153	
154	These run automatically between phases:
155	
156	- **Iter sidecar init** — creates `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/` at iter start so plan + review have a stable destination for per-iter narrative. Top-level files (STRATEGY.md, PROJECT_STATUS.md, task_*.md) stay bounded.
157	- **`sync_leanok`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status.
158	- **`blueprint-doctor`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically detecting orphan chapters, broken `\ref` / `\uses` / `\proves`, and new `axiom` declarations. Findings are written to `logs/iter-NNN/blueprint-doctor.{md,json}` and inlined into the next plan agent's prompt.

67	/-! ## §0. Presheaf-level: the dual of the monoidal unit is the unit
68	
69	Project-local supplement to `PresheafInternalHom.lean`: `PresheafOfModules.dual 𝟙_ ≅ 𝟙_`
70	(the evaluation-at-`1` isomorphism `ℋom(𝟙_, 𝟙_) ≅ 𝟙_`), built over a general single-universe
71	base category.  It feeds `Scheme.Modules.dual_unit_iso` (below) at `R₀ := Y.presheaf`. -/
72	
73	namespace PresheafOfModules
74	
75	open InternalHom Opposite
76	
77	variable {D : Type u} [Category.{u, u} D] {R₀ : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}}
78	
79	/-- **Section equivalence for the dual of the unit.** At an object `X`, endomorphisms of the
80	(restricted) unit `restr X 𝟙_ ⟶ restr X 𝟙_` are identified `R₀(X)`-linearly with `R₀(X)` itself,
81	via evaluation at `1`; the inverse is multiplication by a global scalar (`globalSMul`). The
82	substantive content is `left_inv`: every endomorphism of the unit is multiplication by its value
83	at `1` (proved from `φ`-naturality toward the terminal object of the slice). -/
84	noncomputable def unitDualSectionEquiv (X : Dᵒᵖ) :
85	    letI := internalHomObjModule X.unop
86	      (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))
87	      (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))
88	    (restr X.unop (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))) ⟶
89	        restr X.unop (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))))
90	      ≃ₗ[(R₀.obj (op X.unop) : Type u)] (R₀.obj (op X.unop) : Type u) := by
91	  letI := internalHomObjModule X.unop
92	    (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))
93	    (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))
94	  exact
95	    { toFun := fun φ =>
96	        evalLin (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))) X φ
97	          (1 : ((R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).obj X : Type u))
98	      map_add' := fun φ φ' => rfl
99	      map_smul' := fun c φ => by
100	        exact DFunLike.congr_fun (evalLin_smul _ X c φ)
101	          (1 : ((R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).obj X : Type u))
102	      invFun := fun r =>
103	        globalSMul Over.mkIdTerminal
104	          (restr X.unop (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))) r
105	      left_inv := fun φ => by
106	        ext Y
107	        dsimp only
108	        erw [globalSMul_hom_apply]
109	        have hnat := PresheafOfModules.naturality_apply φ (Over.mkIdTerminal.from Y.unop).op
110	          (1 : ((R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).obj X : Type u))
111	        erw [PresheafOfModules.unit_map_one] at hnat
112	        erw [hnat, smul_eq_mul, mul_one]
113	        rfl
114	      right_inv := fun r => by
115	        change ((globalSMul Over.mkIdTerminal
116	            (restr X.unop
117	              (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))) r).app
118	            (op (Over.mk (𝟙 X.unop)))).hom
119	            (1 : ((R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).obj X : Type u)) = r
120	        rw [globalSMul_hom_apply, termRingMap_terminal]
121	        exact mul_one r }
122	
123	/-- **The presheaf dual of the monoidal unit is the unit**, `PresheafOfModules.dual 𝟙_ ≅ 𝟙_`,
124	assembled sectionwise from `unitDualSectionEquiv` with the evaluation-at-`1` naturality (mirroring
125	`InternalHom.internalHomEval`'s naturality at `M = 𝟙_`). -/
126	noncomputable def dualUnitIsoGen :
127	    PresheafOfModules.dual (R₀ := R₀)
128	        (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))
129	      ≅ 𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) :=
130	  PresheafOfModules.isoMk (fun X => (unitDualSectionEquiv X).toModuleIso)
131	    (fun {X Y} f => by
132	      refine ModuleCat.hom_ext (LinearMap.ext fun φ => ?_)
133	      change evalLin (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))) Y
134	            ((PresheafOfModules.dual
135	              (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))).map f φ)
136	            (1 : ((R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).obj Y : Type u))
137	          = ((𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))).map f).hom
138	              (evalLin (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))) X φ
139	                (1 : ((R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).obj X : Type u)))
140	      have key := PresheafOfModules.naturality_apply
141	        (φ : restr X.unop (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))) ⟶
142	          restr X.unop (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))))
143	        (Over.homMk f.unop : Over.mk f.unop ⟶ Over.mk (𝟙 X.unop)).op
144	        (1 : ((R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).obj X : Type u))
145	      have hrm : (restr X.unop
146	            (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))).map
147	          (Over.homMk f.unop : Over.mk f.unop ⟶ Over.mk (𝟙 X.unop)).op
148	          = (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))).map f := rfl
149	      rw [hrm] at key
150	      erw [PresheafOfModules.unit_map_one] at key
151	      have hAB : (op (Over.mk (𝟙 Y.unop ≫ f.unop)) : (Over X.unop)ᵒᵖ) = op (Over.mk f.unop) :=
152	        congrArg op (congrArg Over.mk (Category.id_comp f.unop))
153	      have homAppHEq : ∀ {A B : (Over X.unop)ᵒᵖ} (_ : A = B), HEq (φ.app A) (φ.app B) := by
154	        intro A B h; subst h; rfl
155	      have hdt : evalLin (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))) Y
156	          ((PresheafOfModules.dual
157	            (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))).map f φ)
158	          = (φ.app (op (Over.mk f.unop))).hom :=
159	        congrArg ModuleCat.Hom.hom (eq_of_heq (homAppHEq hAB))
160	      exact (DFunLike.congr_fun hdt _).trans key)
161	
162	end PresheafOfModules

166	namespace Scheme

168	namespace Modules

170	/-! ## §A. The C-bridge: restriction commutes with the sheaf-level dual -/
171	
172	open Opposite in
173	/-- **Leg-B atomic claim: the lax-monoidal unit `ε` of `restrictScalars` along the open-immersion
174	structure ring iso `(f.appIso W').inv` is an isomorphism.**  Its underlying map is the (bijective)
175	ring map `(f.appIso W').inv.hom`, so `ε` is an iso by `restrictScalars_isIso_ε_of_bijective`
176	(`PresheafInternalHom.lean`) fed the bijectivity from `ConcreteCategory.bijective_of_isIso`.  This
177	is the single load-bearing fact powering `dualUnitRingSwap` (the codomain unit ring swap of leg-B),
178	phrased at the `CommRingCat` carrier so `CommRing` is native (per `analogies/ma-legb262.md`). -/
179	lemma isIso_ε_restrictScalars_appIso {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ X) [IsOpenImmersion f]
180	    (W' : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥Y) :
181	    IsIso (Functor.LaxMonoidal.ε
182	      (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (Scheme.Hom.appIso f W').inv.hom)) :=
183	  restrictScalars_isIso_ε_of_bijective (Scheme.Hom.appIso f W').inv.hom
184	    (CategoryTheory.ConcreteCategory.bijective_of_isIso (Scheme.Hom.appIso f W').inv)
185	
186	open Opposite in
187	/-- **Leg-B: the codomain unit ring-iso swap** `restrictScalars (f.appIso W').inv (𝟙_X(fW')) ⟶
188	𝟙_Y(W')`.  It is the inverse of the lax-monoidal unit `ε (restrictScalars (f.appIso W').inv.hom)`,
189	an isomorphism by `isIso_ε_restrictScalars_appIso`.  The endpoints are written at the canonical
190	`CommRingCat` section carriers `↑(X.presheaf.obj _)` / `↑(Y.presheaf.obj _)` (the `forget₂`-composite
191	carrier breaks `MonoidalCategoryStruct` synthesis, `analogies/ma-legb262.md`); they reconcile by
192	`rfl`/defeq with the `restr`/`𝟙_`-section spellings of `sliceDualTransport`'s `codomainMap` hole. -/
193	noncomputable def dualUnitRingSwap {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ X) [IsOpenImmersion f]
194	    (W' : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥Y) :
195	    (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (Scheme.Hom.appIso f W').inv.hom).obj
196	        (𝟙_ (ModuleCat ↑(X.presheaf.obj (op ((Scheme.Hom.opensFunctor f).obj W'))))) ⟶
197	      𝟙_ (ModuleCat ↑(Y.presheaf.obj (op W'))) :=
198	  haveI := isIso_ε_restrictScalars_appIso f W'
199	  CategoryTheory.inv (Functor.LaxMonoidal.ε
200	    (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (Scheme.Hom.appIso f W').inv.hom))
201	
202	open Opposite in
203	/-- **Leg-B (inverse direction): the unit codomain ring-iso swap for `invFun`** `𝟙_Y(W') ⟶
204	restrictScalars (f.appIso W').inv (𝟙_X(fW'))`.  This is the lax-monoidal unit
205	`ε (restrictScalars (f.appIso W').inv.hom)` ITSELF (not its inverse), the reverse of
206	`dualUnitRingSwap`.  By `isIso_ε_restrictScalars_appIso` it is an isomorphism and is the inverse of
207	`dualUnitRingSwap f W'` (they cancel by `IsIso.inv_hom_id`/`hom_inv_id`). -/
208	noncomputable def dualUnitRingSwapInv {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ X) [IsOpenImmersion f]
209	    (W' : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥Y) :
210	    (𝟙_ (ModuleCat ↑(Y.presheaf.obj (op W')))) ⟶
211	      (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (Scheme.Hom.appIso f W').inv.hom).obj
212	        (𝟙_ (ModuleCat ↑(X.presheaf.obj (op ((Scheme.Hom.opensFunctor f).obj W'))))) :=
213	  Functor.LaxMonoidal.ε (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (Scheme.Hom.appIso f W').inv.hom)
214	
215	open Opposite in
216	/-- `dualUnitRingSwapInv` is a section of `dualUnitRingSwap` (`ε ≫ inv ε = 𝟙`). -/
217	@[simp] lemma dualUnitRingSwapInv_comp_dualUnitRingSwap {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ X)
218	    [IsOpenImmersion f] (W' : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥Y) :
219	    dualUnitRingSwapInv f W' ≫ dualUnitRingSwap f W' = 𝟙 _ := by

5833	  \(M \in \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\). Write \(\alpha\) for the open-immersion
5834	  structure-presheaf natural transformation with components
5835	  \(\alpha_U = (f.\mathtt{appIso}\,U).\mathrm{inv}\), a \emph{\(\mathtt{CommRingCat}\)}-level
5836	  ring isomorphism, and \(\beta = \mathtt{whiskerRight}\,\alpha\,
5837	  (\mathtt{forget}_2\,\mathtt{CommRingCat}\,\mathtt{RingCat})\) for its
5838	  \(\mathtt{RingCat}\)-level shadow, with component
5839	  \(\beta_V \colon \mathcal{O}_Y(V) \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathcal{O}_X(fV)\)
5840	  (\(fV := f.\mathtt{opensFunctor}(V)\)). Then for every open \(V \subseteq Y\) there is an
5841	  \(\mathcal{O}_Y(V)\)-linear isomorphism
5842	  \[
5843	    \bigl((\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta).\mathtt{obj}\,(\mathtt{dual}\,M.\mathtt{val})\bigr)(V)
5844	      \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;
5845	    \bigl(\mathtt{dual}\,((\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta).\mathtt{obj}\,M.\mathtt{val})\bigr)(V),
5846	  \]
5847	  realised as a single \(\mathtt{LinearEquiv.toModuleIso}\) that packages \emph{both} leg (A)
5848	  --- the slice-Hom base-change reindexing across \(f.\mathtt{opensFunctor}\) --- and leg (B)
5849	  --- the unit codomain ring-iso swap. The naturality of the section family in \(W\) has
5850	  \emph{two} distinct parts: the underlying base-morphism uniqueness in
5851	  \((\mathtt{Over}\,V.\mathrm{unop})^{\mathrm{op}}\) is \(\mathtt{Subsingleton.elim}\) (a thin
5852	  poset has at most one inclusion between objects), but the accompanying equation of
5853	  \(\mathcal{O}_Y(V)\)-module maps is a genuine, separate obligation: it is the
5854	  \(\varepsilon\)-naturality of \(\mathtt{restrictScalars}\) along the structure-ring iso
5855	  \(\beta_W\), which \(\mathtt{Subsingleton.elim}\) does \emph{not} discharge.
5856	\end{lemma}
5857	
5858	\begin{proof}
5859	  \uses{lem:internal_hom_isSheaf, lem:restrictscalars_ringiso_dualequiv}
5860	  The isomorphism is produced from an \(\mathcal{O}_Y(V)\)-linear equivalence between the
5861	  left-hand module --- the \(\mathcal{O}_Y(V)\)-module inherited from
5862	  \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta\) --- and the right-hand \(\mathtt{internalHomObjModule}\),
5863	  assembled via \(\mathtt{LinearEquiv.toModuleIso}\), with both module structures
5864	  identified explicitly.
5865	
5866	  \emph{Leg (A): the slice-Hom reindexing, built categorically.} A dual section
5867	  \(\varphi\) of the left-hand value is a morphism \(\mathtt{restr}_{fV}\,M.\mathtt{val} \to
5868	  \mathtt{restr}_{fV}\,\mathbf{1}_X\) over \(\mathtt{Over}\,(fV)\). Its image is the dual
5869	  section over \(\mathtt{Over}\,V\) whose component at \(W \le V\) (with image
5870	  \(fW := f.\mathtt{opensFunctor}(W)\)) is the categorical image
5871	  \[
5872	    (\mathtt{restrictScalars}\,\beta_W).\mathrm{map}\,
5873	      \bigl(\varphi_{(\mathtt{Over.mk}\,(f.\mathtt{opensFunctor}(W \le V)))}\bigr),
5874	  \]
5875	  i.e.\ the component of \(\varphi\) at the \(f\)-image of \(W\), reindexed across
5876	  \(f.\mathtt{opensFunctor}\) by restriction of scalars along \(\beta_W\), and then
5877	  post-composed with the leg-(B) codomain swap below.
5878	
5879	  \emph{Leg (B): the unit codomain ring-iso swap, via the lax-monoidal unit \(\varepsilon\).}
5880	  The codomain of the reindexed component is the restriction of scalars of the unit section,
5881	  which must be carried to the unit section over \(Y\). This is exactly the inverse of the
5882	  lax-monoidal unit comparison of the restriction-of-scalars functor,
5883	  \[
5884	    \mathtt{codomainMap} \;:=\;
5885	      \mathtt{inv}\bigl(\varepsilon(\mathtt{restrictScalars}\,g)\bigr),
5886	      \qquad g := (f.\mathtt{appIso}\,W).\mathrm{inv}.\mathrm{hom},
5887	  \]
5888	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.dualUnitRingSwap}
5889	  where \(g\) is taken at the \emph{\(\mathtt{CommRingCat}\)} level. The unit \(\varepsilon\)
5890	  is invertible because \(g\) is a ring isomorphism
5891	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.isIso_ε_restrictScalars_appIso},
5892	  so \(\varepsilon(\mathtt{restrictScalars}\,g)\) is an isomorphism. The presheaf-level
5893	  identification underlying the unit handling is the dual-of-unit isomorphism for the unit
5894	  section\lean{PresheafOfModules.dualUnitIsoGen}.
5895	
5896	  \emph{Inverse.} The inverse reindexing depends on a set-theoretic down-set fact: because
5897	  \(f\) is an open immersion, \(fV \subseteq \mathrm{range}\,f\), so every open
5898	  \(W'' \le fV\) already lies in the image of \(f.\mathtt{opensFunctor}\), namely
5899	  \(W'' = f.\mathtt{opensFunctor}(f^{-1}W'')\). This is the open-image down-set coverage of an
5900	  \(\mathtt{IsOpenImmersion}\): an open contained in the (open) image of the immersion is the
5901	  functor-image of its own preimage. The project records it as the down-set identity
5902	  \(\iota_!(\iota^{-1}V) = V\) for \(V \le W\) (the helper
5903	  \(\mathtt{image\_preimage\_of\_le}\)\lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.image_preimage_of_le}),
5904	  and it is exactly the lemma the inverse reindexing relies on to define the components on the
5905	  whole down-set of \(fV\).
5906	
5907	  With this in hand the inverse \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.Hom}\) mirrors the forward
5908	  component formula, with two changes: it uses \((f.\mathtt{appIso}\,W'').\mathrm{hom}\) in
5909	  place of its inverse, and its \(\varepsilon\)-codomain swap is
5910	  \(\mathtt{dualUnitRingSwapHom} = \mathtt{inv}\bigl(\varepsilon(\mathtt{restrictScalars}\,
5911	  (f.\mathtt{appIso}\,W'').\mathrm{hom})\bigr)\)\lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.dualUnitRingSwapHom}
5912	  --- that is, the \emph{inverse} of \(\varepsilon\) in the \(\mathrm{hom}\)-direction (where
5913	  \(\varepsilon\) is invertible by
5914	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.isIso_ε_restrictScalars_appIso_hom}), \emph{not}
5915	  \(\varepsilon\) of the \(\mathrm{inv}\)-direction \(\beta\). (The reverse reindex turns an
5916	  \(\mathcal{O}_X(fW'')\)-section map into an \(\mathcal{O}_Y(W'')\)-map, so it must restrict
5917	  along the \(\mathrm{hom}\)-direction ring iso, whose codomain swap is therefore \(\mathtt{inv}\,
5918	  \varepsilon\) of \emph{that} functor.) The whole family is reindexed by the inverse of the
5919	  down-set bijection \(W'' \leftrightarrow f^{-1}W''\) above. The round-trip identities \(\mathtt{left\_inv}\)
5920	  and \(\mathtt{right\_inv}\) then close component-wise by \(\mathtt{Iso.inv\_hom\_id}\) and
5921	  \(\mathtt{Iso.hom\_inv\_id}\) of \(f.\mathtt{appIso}\) (the forward/inverse \(\beta\)-swaps
5922	  cancel) together with the cancellation of the down-set bijection
5923	  \(\iota_!(\iota^{-1}V) = V\) against its inverse.
5924	
5925	  For additivity and \(\mathcal{O}_Y(V)\)-linearity, one must be careful about \emph{which}
5926	  module structure carries the left-hand value. The additive and scalar structure on a dual
5927	  section of the left-hand value
5928	  \(\bigl((\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta).\mathtt{obj}\,(\mathtt{dual}\,M.\mathtt{val})\bigr)(V)\)
5929	  is the \(\mathcal{O}_Y(V)\)-module structure on the internal-hom object
5930	  (\cref{def:presheaf_internal_hom_slice_value}) --- the pointwise module structure in which the
5931	  sum and scalar multiple of dual sections are formed at the terminal object \(\mathrm{id}_{fV}\)
5932	  of the slice over \(fV\). This agrees with, but is \emph{a priori} a distinct presentation of,
5933	  the additive and scalar structure of the underlying presheaf morphism (the pointwise add and
5934	  scalar action on morphisms of presheaves of modules). The verification of additivity and
5935	  \(\mathcal{O}_Y(V)\)-linearity of the transport therefore proceeds in three distinct steps, the
5936	  first of which is a required first move rather than an afterthought:
5937	  \begin{enumerate}
5938	    \item[(i)] \emph{Identify the two module presentations.} The internal-hom module structure's
5939	      addition and scalar action are identified with the underlying pointwise add and scalar action
5940	      on presheaf morphisms. This is a definitional identification of the two
5941	      \(\mathcal{O}_Y(V)\)-module presentations of the same group of dual sections: the
5942	      internal-hom operations, formed at the terminal object of the slice, are precisely the
5943	      pointwise morphism-level operations of the presheaf morphism they package. Until this
5944	      identification is made, the additivity and scalar-compatibility obligations of the transport
5945	      cannot be read off the change-of-rings compatibility, because their sums and scalar multiples
5946	      are taken in the \emph{internal-hom} structure, not the morphism structure.
5947	    \item[(ii)] \emph{Match the X-side and Y-side scalars by the \(\beta\)-naturality ring
5948	      identity.} The scalar acting on the X-side (the pushforward-restricted scalar \(s\) by
5949	      which \(\mathtt{restrictScalars}\,\beta_W\) multiplies the reindexed component) and the
5950	      scalar acting on the Y-side (the \(\mathcal{O}_Y(V)\)-scalar \(c\) of the internal-hom
5951	      action) are \emph{a priori} elements of different rings and cannot be compared directly.
5952	      The bridge is the ring identity
5953	      \[
5954	        s \;=\; (\beta.\mathtt{app}\,W').\mathrm{hom}\, c,
5955	      \]
